Help & Documentation

Welcome to the Blackjack Simulation tool. Here are the available parameters and their meanings:

  • RL Method: Choose between QLearning, Sarsa, and BasicStrategy.
  • Number of Decks: How many decks to use in the simulation. More decks can reduce variance.
  • Max Splits: The maximum number of times a pair can be split (up to 3).

After selecting parameters, click "Run Simulation" to see the results. The output includes:

  • hands_played and shoes_played: Basic counts of how much simulation was conducted.
  • summary: A dictionary with key statistics:
    • win_rate, loss_rate, push_rate: The percentage of outcomes.
    • EV_per_hand: Average profit or loss per hand.
    • final_bankroll: The bankroll after the simulation completes.
    • net_profit: The net gain or loss from the starting bankroll.
    • variance: Variance in profits, useful for understanding volatility.

Use this information to fine-tune your strategy, select different RL methods, or run multiple simulations to compare results.

Back to Home
